default.bodyText: This is the popover body content. It can contain any React nodes — text, links, form fields, or rich markup.

bodyOnly.body: A simple popover without a title. Good for brief contextual details.

withHeaderNoClose.title: Filter options
withHeaderNoClose.body: Use the header to label the popover when the context isn't obvious from the trigger alone.

withHeaderAndClose.title: Account settings
withHeaderAndClose.body: The close button (×) in the header provides a secondary dismiss target alongside Escape and click-outside.

withFooter.title: Confirm action
withFooter.body: The footer typically holds primary and secondary action buttons. Use it when the user needs to confirm or cancel.

controlled.title: Display settings
controlled.body: Controlled state is managed externally via the open + onOpenChange props. This enables imperative control from a parent component.

infoOnly.body: A minimal body-only popover triggered by an icon button. Great for tooltips that need to hold interactive content.

darkMode.title: Dark mode popover
darkMode.body: Panel uses --color-surface-raised which resolves to a dark surface in dark mode.
darkMode.footerBody: Footer and header dividers are subtler in dark mode to avoid harsh contrast.

playground.title: Playground popover
playground.body: Use the controls panel to adjust placement, toggle the close button, and switch to a controlled open state.

accessibility.title: Keyboard accessible dialog
accessibility.body: This panel has role='dialog', aria-modal='true', and aria-labelledby pointing to the heading. Focus is trapped inside while open and returns to the trigger on close.
accessibility.bodyOnlyText: When no title is provided, aria-labelledby is omitted. Consider adding an aria-label directly on the trigger for SR context.
accessibility.note: Test keyboard: Tab/Shift+Tab wraps within the panel. Escape closes and returns focus to the trigger. The trigger button carries aria-expanded and aria-controls.

edge_cases.very_long_title: This is an extremely long title that should be truncated with an ellipsis when it overflows the available header width
edge_cases.rich_body: Body can contain bold text, links, or form elements. The panel has overflow-hidden and a max-width token.
edge_cases.no_title_no_footer: Minimal popover — only body content, no header, no footer.
edge_cases.nested_interactive: Body with a text input or button — focus trap keeps Tab/Shift+Tab inside the panel.
